concerns.text: Task T031 implements 'Maximum Statistic' p-value calculation, but Task T040 (which updates spec.md to replace FR-009 Benjamini-Hochberg with Maximum Statistic) is scheduled in Phase 6. This creates a semantic ordering violation where the implementation (consumer) proceeds before the requirement definition (producer) is corrected, violating the 'Producer before consumer' principle for the spec artifact.

Task T022 (compute_hemisphere_cl) depends on T021 (split_hemispheres) as noted, but T022 also implicitly depends on the masked/downgraded map produced by US1 (T011-T013). The dependency section lists US2 depending on US1, but the task list does not explicitly order T022 after T013 completion, risking parallel execution attempts where the input artifact (masked map) is not yet ready.

Task T012 description conflates two metrics: 'verify ≥95% sky retention' vs '≥95% of masked pixels are excluded'. The spec requires ≥95% of the *sky* to remain unmasked (implying <5% masked). The task text '≥95% of masked pixels are excluded' is ambiguous and could be misinterpreted as excluding 95% of the *entire* sky (leaving only 5%), which contradicts the spec. Clarify to 'verify ≥95% sky retention'.

Task T042 targets 'A significant reduction' in runtime. This is a vague success condition that cannot be ordered or verified against a concrete metric. Replace with a specific target (e.g., 'reduce Monte Carlo loop runtime by 20%') or remove the performance target from the task description to avoid ambiguity in execution ordering.
